title: The pointed-arch tablet is the universal mark of inscribed law. The .precept file IS this tablet: self-contained, complete, and immutable. The three bright rules on its face are the precepts themselves — not scattered logic, not runtime guards, but laws carved into the definition that hold forever.
conceptualBridge: The law tablet means a Precept definition is a self-contained contract because its rules are inscribed once and then hold structurally.
decision: Removed: user manually rejected (weight=0, review status=rejected, note: 'looks rude, doesn't really say anything to me about precept'). Law-tablet direction is temporarily closed.
